DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SOUTHERN GLAZER'S WINE AND SPIRITS LLC'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the first, second and fourth causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Alexander Andreas and Boom Boom Room Inc. is OVERRULED as to the first and second causes of action for fraud by intentional misrepresentation and concealment and SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of fair dealing. While the SAC, like the FAC, is far from a model of clarity and violates the basic rule of pleading codified at CCP 425.10(a), the SAC adequately puts defendants on notice of the intentional misrepresentations and concealments of material facts they are alleged to have committed. Sustaining the demurrers to the fraud claims without leave to amend would impermissibly preclude plaintiffs from pursuing potentially meritorious claims, while the recent history of this case suggests that sustaining the demurrer to those claims with leave to amend is likely to yield little of value and only result in more ultimately inefficacious motion practice. Bowing to the shortness of life, if not litigation, it is time to move past the pleading stage as to the fraud claims. If they have not already done so, defendants presumably will propound discovery directed to learning all facts that plaintiffs possess regarding the fraud claims, which will provide defendants with any further illumination plaintiffs are able to muster on those claims. Plaintiffs' re-styled implied covenant claim fails for the same reason that their implied covenant claim alleged in the FAC also failed: the implied covenant claim is based on the same facts and alleges the same breach (i.e. plaintiffs overpaid Southern Glazer per the parties' contract) as the breach of contract claim. Because it does not appear that plaintiffs are able to allege a viable implied covenant claim, they are denied leave to do so. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Christopher E. Andreas of the law offices of Christopher E. Andreas (415-828-5024) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Susan J. Harriman of the law offices of Keker Van Nest and Peters LLP (415-676-2213) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party, Glazer's Wine & Spirits

Attorney, Dean D. Paik of the law offices of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell (415-956-2828) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring, Ron Armada

The demurrers are argued concurrently and submitted by all sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court reverses the tentative ruling only as to the 7th cause of action which is overruled. The Court otherwise confirms the tentative ruling as to all causes of action.

DEFENDANT SOUTHERN GLAZER'S WINE AND SPIRITS LLC's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the first through fourth and sixth through eighth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Alexander Andreas and Boom Boom Room Inc. is: a) SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to the first, second and sixth causes of action for misrepresentation, concealment and breach of the implied covenant; b) SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the third, fourth and ninth causes of action for conversion, breach of common law fiduciary duty and tortious interference with contract; and c) OVERRULED as to the seventh and eighth causes of action for money had and received and open book account. While the two fraud claims are not barred by the economic loss rule per Robinson Helicopter, they are not alleged with the required specificity, Plaintiffs need to allege with precision what misrepresentations were made to them, when they were made and how they relied on them. They also need to allege with precision what facts were concealed from them, for what period of time, and how they relied on the concealment of those facts. Fraud committed by Mr. Armada as an employee of Southern Glazer is attributable to Southern Glazer. The conversion claim fails as a matter of law because the plaintiffs have not, nor could they credibly, allege that the money that is the subject of this claim is readily identifiable. The breach of fiduciary duty claim fails as a matter of law because the parties were not in a fiduciary relationship. As an employee of defendant Southern Glazer Wine & Spirits LLC, Mr. Armada cannot be liable for interference with the contract between plaintiffs and Southern Glazer. The implied covenant claim is deficient because it alleges the identical breach as the breach of contract claim. The money had and received claim fails as a matter of law because such claim does not lie for an alleged overpayment procured by a misstatement. (CACI 370). An applicable common count, which is adequately alleged as an alternative to the breach of contract claim, is a claim for open book account. (CACI 372). Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No: 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
